body.body:
From D113 (closed as ticketed). Akien 2026-04-14: 'if we don't have a list of core principles in the master architecture doc, ticket that we need it.'

## Current state

A partial principles list exists in lab/design_docs_for_igor/dev_process.dsb at line 53 under ARCHITECTURAL_PRINCIPLES. Six items:
  1. everything_is_memory — no special cases; if in doubt, make it a memory type
  2. stateless_by_design — hot-reloadable modules hold no state; state lives in DB
  3. single_choke_points — cortex owns all memory r/w; gateway owns all inference; never bypass
  4. encapsulation — each object owns everything about its structure
  5. minimalist — three similar lines > premature abstraction; don't design for hypothetical future
  6. no_backwards_compat_hacks — if unused, delete it; don't rename to _old

**Problems:**
- It's in the wrong file (dev_process is process-shaped; principles are architecture-shaped)
- The master architecture doc (architecture_root.dsb) doesn't reference or contain it
- The memory palace has no principles node — so Igor can't query his own architectural principles at runtime
- Both files were last touched 2026-04-06 (a week ago) — drifting toward stale
- D113 (degrade gracefully) was filed as a 'core principle everywhere' but never landed on the list

## Scope

1. **Decide canonical location.** Probably architecture_root.dsb (CORE_PRINCIPLES section) AND a memory_palace node at theigors/architecture/principles/* with one node per principle. Both: humans read the dsb, Igor reads the palace at runtime.
2. **Migrate the existing 6 principles** from dev_process.dsb to the canonical location(s). Leave a pointer in dev_process if the moved-out doc still needs to reference them.
3. **Add D113 as the 7th principle: degrade_gracefully** = any dependency failure (service down, DB unreachable, inference tier unavailable) triggers habit chain for recovery + graceful tier fallback; not special-case code, same habit machinery as everything else.
4. **Audit other principle-shaped decisions.** Walk the decisions table for items tagged or shaped as 'core principle' that should also land here. Anything that says 'X everywhere' or 'always Y' is a candidate.
5. **Document the rule for additions.** New principles get added to the canonical location at the moment of decision, not deferred to a future cleanup pass. (This ticket exists because D113 was filed as 'core principle' in 2026-04-11 and sat in defined for 3 days.)

## Why this matters

Same failure mode as D112 SystemGateway today: load-bearing item filed as a decision, not visible anywhere actionable, forgotten. Akien thought the principles list was there. It partly was, partly wasn't, and the new principle (D113) never landed on any version of it.

Under the cockpit/COA framing from this morning's Igor conversation, principles are EXACTLY the kind of thing that should be on a cockpit instrument — Igor should be able to query his own principles when reasoning about a design choice. That's only possible if they live in the matrix (palace), not just in a file.

## Existing work to check

- lab/design_docs_for_igor/architecture_root.dsb — current master arch doc (stale, last bumped 2026-03-13)
- lab/design_docs_for_igor/dev_process.dsb — has the partial ARCHITECTURAL_PRINCIPLES section
- lab/theigors/* repo echo of memory_palace — where the new palace nodes would land
- lab/claudecode/palace_sync.py — auto-sync mechanism for palace ↔ repo echo

## Out of scope

- Big rewrite of architecture_root.dsb — just add the section
- Re-litigating any of the existing 6 principles
- New principles beyond D113 (open the door for the audit pass but don't pre-decide the audit results)

## Provenance

D113 (Akien 2026-04-11, surfaced and closed as ticketed 2026-04-14)

## Added 2026-04-14 — additional candidate principles

While walking the decisions table, two more principle-shaped items surfaced that should land alongside D113 (degrade_gracefully) when this ticket sprints:

**Principle: any_node_can_be_a_parent** — In the tree-based architecture, any node can become a parent simply by adding child nodes. Schema doesn't change. If a single datum (a checkbox) needs to become a dropdown list, you just tweak metadata and add children. Use this freedom: prefer adding children to widening the parent's metadata. Captures one of the core advantages of the matrix over relational tables. (Akien 2026-04-14, surfaced during D124 walk — context was hardware inventory where storage might evolve from one disk to many.)

**Principle: timestamp_id_format_uniform** — Use the D256 timestamp ID format (yyyymmdd.hhmmssuuuuuu.xxxxxxx) as the canonical id shape across all node types. Don't introduce parallel id encodings. Already in use by instance_log, GOAL_, PRA_, PR_GOAL_*. (Akien 2026-04-14, surfaced during D011 close — base-34 alternative was rejected because the timestamp format already covers unique+sortable+age-encoded.)

When the audit pass runs (step 4 of this ticket), look for more principle-shaped decisions to add. The audit should also catch any not yet promoted from defined to implemented or ticketed.

## Added 2026-04-14 — D155 candidate principle

**Principle: parent_id_as_training_wheels** — The `parent_id` field on a memory is a one-tree-per-node simplification, training wheels for the case when a node has obvious primary parentage. Real tree membership is plural — nodes belong to many trees via `interpretive_edges`. Activation trails (tails) record which tree's traversal reached a node *this time*, so the "active parent" is contextual, not stored. **Don't be tempted to widen parent_id into a list** when a node accumulates multiple parents — use interpretive_edges. The single field is a convenience over the real multi-edge model. (D155, surfaced 2026-04-14.)

## Update 2026-04-14 evening — CP patterns are the axioms, principles are the theorems

Akien conversation clarified a level distinction I should make explicit in this work. The 6 CP patterns (genesis seed memories CP1-CP6) and the principles are at DIFFERENT ontological levels:

- **CP patterns = axioms.** Epistemic/ontological priors that constrain everything downstream. Inherited directly from Akien's own epistemology via genesis seeding. Cannot be derived from anything else — they're the starting points.
- **Principles (this ticket) = theorems.** Derived behavioral + architectural rules. Every principle should be derivable from one or more CP patterns. Any proposed principle that violates a CP pattern should be rejected.

**For the architecture_root.dsb CORE_PRINCIPLES section:** add a pointer at the top of the section pointing at the CP memories (`CP1-CP6` in the live DB) as "the axiom layer these principles derive from." Palace node at `theigors/architecture/principles` gets the same pointer.

**Akien's own epistemology (surfaced 2026-04-14 evening) explained:**
- CP1 "I don't know" = every fact is a current best guess
- CP2 "FAIL = Further Advance In Learning" = mistakes are safe, they're how learning happens
- CP3 "There's always a why" = causality is investigable, experiments are tractable
- CP4 "Make everything suck less for everybody" = aspirational direction
- CP5 "Assume and respect the possibility of experience in all systems" = why biomimicry matters
- CP6 "The world is not a safe place. We have to build and care for safety as we go." = anti-PTSD vaccination by construction; no assumed-safety prior to collapse

**Every engram, every habit, every principle should be checkable against these.** If it violates any of the six, it's suspect. This becomes a registered audit check (via T-audit-add-checks) once engrams are identifiable as first-class objects.
body.title: Add core principles list to master architecture doc + memory palace
body.result: e993bd20 — CORE_PRINCIPLES section added to architecture_root.dsb (10 principles total: 6 carried from dev_process.dsb + degrade_gracefully from D113 + 3 surfaced today). dev_process.dsb pointer-only. memory_palace seeded with one node per principle under theigors/architecture/principles/* + parent + theigors/architecture root. palace_sync ran (44 palace nodes total, was 32). lab/theigors echo committed.
